title: Encoding Auditory Space in Marmoset Cortex

summary.hook: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space.
summary.summary_30s: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space.

audience_versions.clinician: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space. Spatial tuning sharpens progressively toward the contralateral pole, culminating in a dense cluster of narrowly tuned neurons at –90°. For clinicians, the main value is whether the findings could inform care decisions, screening, workflow, or patient communication.
audience_versions.community: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space. Auditory cortex encodes spatial direction with non-uniform tuning sharpness that peaks contralaterally. For community partners, the key question is who benefits, who might be left out, and how the work could translate into real-world impact.
audience_versions.leader: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space. Auditory cortex encodes spatial direction with non-uniform tuning sharpness that peaks contralaterally. For leaders, the main lens is whether this work is ready for scale, support, implementation, or further investment within brain, behavior & aging.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360° when vision primarily covers frontal space.
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Neurophysiology / observational experimental recording study with cross-modal manipulation (auditory stimuli with and without frontal visual image).. * Animals: 4 marmosets
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Spatial tuning sharpens progressively toward the contralateral pole, culminating in a dense cluster of narrowly tuned neurons at –90°.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Auditory cortex encodes spatial direction with non-uniform tuning sharpness that peaks contralaterally.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: How do you define and compute the SRF for each unit from the 360° broadband-noise stimulus set?

key_numbers: Sharply tuned neurons preferred contralateral direction centered around −90° (direction–sharpness coupling; F-test...
Linear regression model significant for 155/166 units (p<0.05); k quartiles [0.67, 0.88, 1.13], b quartiles [0.02, 0.05...
This study asks how auditory cortex in foveate primates (common marmoset) encodes sound direction across the full 360°...
Using single-unit recordings in auditory cortex under head-fixed conditions (no auditory task) with broadband noise...
takeaways: Spatial tuning sharpens progressively toward the contralateral pole, culminating in a dense cluster of narrowly tuned neurons at –90°.
Peaking tuning direction does not change with frontal vision, especially for narrowly tuned neurons (no remapping of best azimuth).
A frontal wide-field image multiplicatively scales firing rates but leaves best azimuth unchanged (cross-modal gain field rather than spatial realignment).
